Title: M1 — Infrastructure Ready
Description: Keycloak running on Docker Compose with PostgreSQL, nginx reverse proxy configured, TLS active on staging and production domains. Health check endpoint responding.

Title: M2 — Realm & Identity Configured
Description: Internal and external realms created. Password policies set. Realm-level roles defined. User profile attributes confirmed. app-provisioner service client created.

Title: M3 — Users Migrated
Description: All existing users bulk-imported into Keycloak with bcrypt hashes. keycloak_id backfilled into all app databases. Zero NULL keycloak_id for active users. Verified on staging first.

Title: M4 — Wave 1 Apps Integrated
Description: 1–2 lowest-risk internal apps integrated with Keycloak SSO. OIDC flow working end-to-end. JWT validation on backend confirmed. Local auth still active in parallel.

Title: M5 — Wave 2 Apps Integrated
Description: All remaining internal apps integrated. Client roles defined per app. Access control via JWT claims verified for all internal users.

Title: M6 — Auth Facade Live
Description: Auth facade deployed. Old mobile app versions routing through ROPC bridge to Keycloak. Version gate active. New mobile app using PKCE flow. Both paths verified.

Title: M7 — Wave 3 Apps Integrated
Description: All customer-facing apps integrated with Keycloak external realm. End-to-end tested with real external users. Mobile new version live in app stores.

Title: M8 — Local Auth Decommissioned
Description: Local auth disabled on all apps. Password columns removed from app databases. Unused auth dependencies removed from codebases. SSO is the only login method.

Title: M9 — Mobile Facade Sunset
Description: Auth facade returns 410 Gone for old mobile versions. Deprecation notice shown from month 3–4. Hard cutoff enforced at month 6–9 based on adoption analytics.
